page: Vultr voc-g-4c-16gb-80s-amd is a general-purpose virtual server instance belonging to the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It features 4 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided by an 80 GB local NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this server type.

Performance benchmarks show a mix of top-tier single-core capabilities and average multi-core throughput. In stress-ng single-core CPU testing, the instance achieves a top-tier score of 3435.28, placing it in the top 10% of tested servers. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression also reaches top-tier performance at 14.72 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance is more moderate, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 13682.44 and a PassMark CPU score of 11760.94, both landing in the average range. Memory write bandwidth for large blocks is strong at 113.87 GB/sec, while read bandwidth remains average. Conversely, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and static web serving throughput are relatively weak, performing in the bottom 25% of tested servers.

The shared CPU allocation presents a performance tradeoff, as resource contention may affect multi-threaded workloads, which is reflected in the weaker multi-threaded decompression and database operation benchmarks. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a balanced resource density of 4 GB RAM per vCPU alongside fast NVMe storage, making it a cost-effective option for workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. This instance is best suited for general-purpose applications, single-threaded processing tasks, lightweight web serving, and small-scale LLM inference using models under 1B parameters.
description: Vultr voc-g-4c-16gb-80s-amd is a general-purpose virtual server from the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 4 shared vCPUs on an AMD EPYC Genoa processor (x86_64 architecture) running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, an 80 GB local NVMe SSD, and one public IPv4 address. Benchmarks reveal top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core scoring 3435.28 and single-threaded bzip3 decompression reaching 14.72 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded performance and database operations are weaker, reflecting the shared CPU allocation. This server offers a balanced resource profile, making it a qualitatively cost-effective choice for general-purpose workloads, single-threaded processing, and lightweight applications that do not require dedicated CPU resources.

tagline: A balanced virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC Genoa processors and fast NVMe storage for single-threaded and general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 4 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs at 2.0 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB RAM and 80 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance
Features a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio for general-purpose use
Best suited for single-threaded tasks, lightweight applications, and general-purpose workloads
